steps.title: Understand the relationship between sentences
steps.content: The previous sentence establishes one mandate of the Twentieth Amendment: presidential inaugurations on January 20. The blank introduces a second, separate mandate: senators and representatives sworn in on January 3. These two requirements are coordinate—both imposed by the same amendment—so the transition must signal addition, not contrast, causation, or specification.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Instead,: “Instead” signals a substitute or alternative, implying the second requirement replaces the first. Both requirements coexist. ✗
  • Choice B — For instance,: “For instance” signals an example of the preceding idea, but the senators’ swearing-in is a separate mandate, not an illustration of presidential inauguration. ✗
  • Choice C — Specifically,: “Specifically” signals a narrowing or elaboration of the prior sentence, but January 3 is not a detail within the January 20 inauguration mandate. ✗
  • Choice D — In addition,: “In addition” signals a second, coordinate requirement of the same amendment—exactly what the sentence presents. ✓
That matches Choice D — In addition,.
